content.full_text:
June 9, 1978

Senator John Briggs  
Room 4068  
State Capitol  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Senator Briggs:

In accordance with your request I am supplying you with the text of my conversation of January 6, 1978, with Mr. Leon Jones.

My notes for the referenced date indicate that Mr. Jones identified himself to me as "treasurer of a committee and a CPA."

The precise text of my notes of the conversation is as follows:

"Candidate received $100,000 from a referendum committee. Deposited in his campaign committee. Then a few days later he wrote a check back to the referendum committee. (After the first of the year.) The money had been lent originally to the referendum committee, they repaid it, and then he re-lent it. Is there anything improper about these transactions?

"No, as long as they are fully reported at the appropriate times."

Sincerely yours,

John D. Giansello  
Political Reform Analyst  
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
